rule_description: The standard Advance Reservation Period (ARP) for Indian Railways is 60 days excluding the date of journey. Online general reservations open at 08:00 AM IST on the booking opening date. For intermediate stations, the booking date is calculated based on the train's departure from its originating station.
Foreign tourists can book railway tickets up to 365 days in advance of the journey date.
Certain day-time express and short-distance trains may have a shorter advance reservation period of 30 days instead of the standard 60 days.
Tatkal ticket booking for AC classes (1A, 2A, 3A, CC, 3E) opens at 10:00 AM IST, one day before the journey date. Aadhaar-linked OTP authentication is mandatory for online Tatkal bookings.
Tatkal ticket booking for non-AC classes (Sleeper, Second Sitting) opens at 11:00 AM IST, one day before the journey date.
Authorized IRCTC agents are barred from booking Tatkal tickets during the first 30 minutes of the booking window (10:00 AM – 10:30 AM for AC classes; 11:00 AM – 11:30 AM for non-AC classes).

Confirmed Tatkal tickets are non-refundable upon voluntary cancellation by the passenger. However, waitlisted Tatkal tickets that remain waitlisted after chart preparation are eligible for automatic refund as per standard rules.
Reserved tickets are booked in advance and guarantee a specific seat or berth. These can be booked online via IRCTC website/app or at railway PRS (Passenger Reservation System) counters.

Unreserved tickets are for General (GN) class travel. They can be purchased at station counters, via ATVM (Automatic Ticket Vending Machines), or through the UTS (Unreserved Ticketing System) mobile app. They do not guarantee a specific seat.

Platform tickets allow non-travelers to enter railway platforms to see off or receive passengers. They are valid for 2 hours from the time of issue. Cost ranges from Rs.10 to Rs.50 depending on the station. Entering a platform without a ticket is an offense punishable by penalty.
The default quota for most passengers. The largest allocation of seats/berths in any train.
Emergency last-minute quota that opens one day before departure. Has additional premium charges over the base fare. Available in most classes except 1A on many trains.
Similar to Tatkal but with dynamic pricing. Fares increase based on demand. Available on select trains.
A quota reserved exclusively for female passengers traveling alone or with children below 12 years. Available on select trains.
Reserved for serving defence personnel. Requires valid defence identity card and authorization.
A special quota reserved for foreign nationals and NRIs. Bookings can be made up to 365 days in advance. Payment accepted in foreign currency or equivalent.
A quota for senior citizens (men aged 60+ and women aged 58+) and persons with disabilities. Provides automatic allotment of lower berths subject to availability.
A quota for unemployed youth aged 15-45 years. Available on select trains at discounted rates.
Reserved for railway employees traveling on duty. Requires valid duty pass documentation.
Reserved for Members of Parliament. Requires valid identification as a sitting MP.

rule_title: Cancellation More Than 48 Hours Before Departure
rule_description: Flat cancellation charge per passenger applies. AC First/Executive Class: Rs.240, AC 2-Tier: Rs.200, AC 3-Tier/AC Chair Car: Rs.180, Sleeper Class: Rs.120, Second Class: Rs.60. Maximum refund is given.
25% of the total fare is deducted as cancellation charge, subject to the minimum flat cancellation charge applicable for the class.
50% of the total fare is deducted as cancellation charge, subject to the minimum flat cancellation charge applicable for the class.

rule_title: Cancellation Less Than 4 Hours Before Departure
rule_description: No refund is admissible for confirmed tickets if not cancelled before 4 hours of scheduled departure. A TDR (Ticket Deposit Receipt) may be filed in exceptional circumstances.

If an e-ticket remains fully waitlisted after the preparation of the final chart, it is automatically cancelled by the system and a full refund (minus clerkage charge) is processed to the original payment method.
RAC tickets can be cancelled up to 30 minutes before the scheduled departure of the train. Flat cancellation charges apply as per the class of travel.
If Indian Railways cancels a train, all passengers holding valid tickets are entitled to a full refund without any cancellation charges. No TDR filing is required.

Counter tickets (booked at PRS counters) can be cancelled online if a valid mobile number was provided at the time of booking. Otherwise, they must be cancelled at a PRS counter within the permitted time limits.

The most premium and expensive class in Indian Railways. Features private cabins with 2 or 4 berths with lockable doors, providing maximum privacy and comfort. Bedding, meals, and individual charging points are included. Coaches are fully air-conditioned.

Comfortable overnight AC travel class with open bays. Each bay has 4 berths (2 lower, 2 upper) plus 2 side berths. Each berth has a privacy curtain, reading light, and charging point. Bedding is provided.
A popular medium-budget AC class. Each bay has 6 berths (2 lower, 2 middle, 2 upper) plus 2 side berths. Air-conditioned with bedding provided. Charging points available.
Similar to AC 3-Tier but more economical. Each bay has 6 berths plus 2 side berths (middle berth on the side as well, making 9 berths per section in some configurations). Available on newer trains.
Air-conditioned seating class primarily used for shorter day journeys. Features comfortable reclining seats arranged in rows. Available on Shatabdi, Jan Shatabdi, and Vande Bharat Express trains.
Premium seating class with more spacious and comfortable seats than CC. Available on select premium trains like Shatabdi Express and Vande Bharat Express. Includes complimentary meals on some trains.
The most common non-AC class for overnight travel. Budget-friendly with 8 berths per bay (same layout as 3A but without air conditioning). No bedding provided. Fans and windows for ventilation.
The most economical reserved seating class. Non-AC with basic seats. Primarily used for short-distance day travel. Available on select trains.
The most basic and cheapest way to travel on Indian Railways. Tickets are unreserved and do not guarantee a specific seat. Coaches are typically at the front and rear of the train.
Air-conditioned glass-roofed coaches offering panoramic views. Available on scenic routes. Features rotatable seats and large observation windows on the roof and sides.
AC First Class (1A) passengers are allowed 70 kg of luggage free of charge. Maximum limit with extra charge is 150 kg.
AC 2-Tier (2A) and First Class (FC) passengers are allowed 50 kg of luggage free of charge. Maximum limit with extra charge is 100 kg.
AC 3-Tier (3A) and AC Chair Car (CC) passengers are allowed 40 kg of luggage free of charge. Maximum limit with extra charge is 40 kg.
Sleeper Class (SL) passengers are allowed 40 kg of luggage free of charge. Maximum limit with extra charge is 80 kg.
Second Class (2S) and General (GN) passengers are allowed 35 kg of luggage free of charge. Maximum limit with extra charge is 70 kg.
Trunks, suitcases, and boxes exceeding 100 cm x 60 cm x 25 cm must be booked in the brake van. For AC 3-Tier and AC Chair Car, the size limit is 55 cm x 45 cm x 22.5 cm.

Inflammable materials, explosive substances, dangerous articles including gas cylinders, acids, and corrosive substances are strictly prohibited on passenger trains. Carrying such items is a punishable offense under the Railways Act.
The first reservation chart is typically prepared 8 to 10 hours before the scheduled departure of the train from its originating station.
For trains departing early in the morning (before 08:00 AM), the reservation chart is generally prepared the previous night.
A second reservation chart is prepared 30 minutes to a few hours before the train's departure to allocate unutilized berths, accommodate last-minute changes, and confirm RAC/waitlisted passengers.

If all passengers on an e-ticket remain in waitlist status after final chart preparation, the ticket is automatically cancelled. Passengers are NOT allowed to board the train with a fully waitlisted e-ticket. Doing so will result in being treated as a ticketless traveler with applicable fine.

RAC (Reservation Against Cancellation) ticket guarantees you can board the train but does not ensure a full berth. You will be allotted a seat to share with another RAC passenger. RAC passengers are upgraded to confirmed berths as cancellations occur.
If at least one passenger on the same PNR has a confirmed or RAC status, the entire ticket remains valid and all passengers (including those still on the waitlist) are permitted to board the train.

At least one passenger booked on a reserved ticket must carry an original government-issued photo ID during the journey. Valid documents include: Aadhaar Card (including e-Aadhaar/m-Aadhaar), Passport, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Photo ID issued by Central/State Government, Student ID with photograph, Nationalized Bank Passbook with photograph, Credit Card with laminated photograph, and Photo ID cards issued by PSUs or Municipal bodies.

Failure to produce a valid original ID proof during the journey when asked by the TTE will result in the passenger being treated as traveling without a valid ticket. Applicable fines and penalties will be levied.

Senior citizen concessions (formerly 40% for men aged 60+ and 50% for women aged 58+) were suspended in March 2020 and have not been reinstated as of 2025. However, senior citizens are still eligible for automatic allotment of lower berths subject to availability.

Concessions are available for students participating in tournaments recognized by schools or universities, and for other specific educational purposes. Detailed rules are in the IRCA Coaching Tariff. Requires valid student ID and authorization letter.
Concessions for recognized freedom fighters are governed by official railway policy. Requires specific documentation issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs.

Persons with disabilities (blindness, orthopedic disability, mental retardation, deaf-mute) are eligible for 50% to 75% concession depending on the type of disability. Requires disability certificate from a government hospital.

Waiting rooms are for bona fide passengers with a valid ticket. Access is often restricted by class of travel (AC or non-AC). Passengers may access waiting rooms up to 3 hours before train departure or after arrival. Registration in the waiting room register may be required.

Only passengers with a confirmed or RAC ticket (valid PNR) can book retiring rooms. Rooms can be booked for a minimum of 3 hours and maximum of 48 hours. Booking is available online via IRCTC website or mobile app. A valid government-issued ID is required at check-in.

Cloakrooms are available for passengers with a valid journey ticket at the boarding or destination station. Luggage can be stored for a minimum of 3 hours and up to 30 days (varies by station). Luggage must be securely locked and tagged. Always retain the deposit receipt.

Catering services are managed by IRCTC through onboard pantry cars and static units (food plazas, stalls). Meals must meet FSSAI hygiene standards. Passengers can order food via the e-catering portal (ecatering.irctc.co.in). Complaints can be reported via the 138 helpline or @IRCATERING on Twitter.
On Vande Bharat Express and certain premium trains, passengers who have opted for meals are entitled to complimentary food if the train is significantly delayed (2 hours or more).

Traveling without a valid ticket or with an improper ticket is an offense under the Railways Act, 1989. The penalty includes the fare for the distance traveled plus an additional charge (excess charge) which varies by class.

rule_title: Fine for WL Passengers in Reserved Coaches
rule_description: Passengers with waitlisted tickets (both online and offline) are not permitted to board reserved coaches (AC or Sleeper). Fine of Rs.440 for AC coaches and Rs.250 for Sleeper coaches, plus the applicable fare.
Pulling the alarm chain without valid reason is a punishable offense under Section 141 of the Railways Act. Penalty can include imprisonment up to one year or fine up to Rs.1000 or both.
Smoking is strictly prohibited in all air-conditioned coaches, waiting rooms, and enclosed areas of railway stations. Violation can attract a fine of Rs.200 under the Railways Act.

The Station Master is the primary executive in charge of a railway station. They are the only authority permitted to grant Line Clear or authorize a train's departure/arrival. They manage signal clearances and ensure smooth, punctual train movement. They supervise all station staff (porters, pointsmen, etc.) and are responsible for station discipline and cleanliness. They must ensure all signals, points, and station machinery are in proper working order and compliant with the General Rules (G&SR) and Station Working Rules (SWR). They conduct regular inspections of cabins, signals, level crossing gates, and the station yard.
Station Masters operate under the General Rules (G&SR) of Indian Railways and specific Station Working Rules (SWR) applicable to their station.

The TTE is a commercial department official responsible for: Verifying that all passengers hold valid tickets and travel in their designated berths/seats. Issuing Excess Fare Tickets (EFTs) and collecting fines from ticketless or improperly ticketed travelers. Allocating vacant berths to RAC and waitlisted passengers. Addressing passenger complaints regarding amenities. Maintaining records of passengers and earnings. Ensuring decorum, cleanliness, and adherence to train rules (such as locking vestibule doors at night for safety). Reporting daily activities to the Station Master or Control Office.
TTEs follow the commercial duty lists issued by the Railway Board, which are subject to periodic updates.

The RPF is an armed force of the Union tasked with: Protecting railway property from theft or vandalism. Ensuring passenger safety by escorting trains, patrolling platforms, and maintaining law and order. Investigating and prosecuting offenders under the Railways Act (1989) and the Railway Property (Unlawful Possession) Act (1966). Preventing human trafficking (especially of women and children). Providing aid to vulnerable individuals found on railway premises.

The RPF has legal authority to arrest, investigate, and prosecute offenders. They operate under the RPF Act, 1957, and are empowered to enforce security and safety regulations across the entire railway jurisdiction.

The Government Railway Police (GRP) is a state police force that handles criminal matters on railway premises. Unlike the RPF which protects railway property, the GRP deals with crimes against passengers such as theft, robbery, assault, and murder. Both work in coordination for overall railway security.

The Guard is in charge of the train during its journey. Responsible for ensuring the safe running of the train, maintaining the train's schedule, managing emergencies en route, checking brakes, coordinating with the driver and station masters, and maintaining the train journal. The Guard gives the all-clear signal for the train to depart.

The Loco Pilot (Train Driver) operates the locomotive. Responsible for driving the train safely at prescribed speeds, observing all signals, maintaining alertness, checking locomotive condition before departure, reporting any mechanical issues, and following speed restrictions in curves, bridges, and construction zones. Must pass periodic medical and competency tests.

Track Maintainers (formerly called Gangmen) are responsible for the inspection, maintenance, and repair of railway tracks. They patrol assigned track sections daily, check for rail fractures, loose fittings, alignment issues, and ensure drainage. They report any unsafe conditions to the section engineer.

Indian Railways is divided into 18 zones: Central Railway (CR), East Central Railway (ECR), East Coast Railway (ECoR), Eastern Railway (ER), Metro Railway Kolkata, North Central Railway (NCR), North Eastern Railway (NER), North Western Railway (NWR), Northeast Frontier Railway (NFR), Northern Railway (NR), South Central Railway (SCR), South East Central Railway (SECR), South Eastern Railway (SER), South Western Railway (SWR), Southern Railway (SR), West Central Railway (WCR), Western Railway (WR), and Konkan Railway (KR).

Each railway zone has its own headquarters: CR-Mumbai, ECR-Hajipur, ECoR-Bhubaneswar, ER-Kolkata, NCR-Prayagraj, NER-Gorakhpur, NWR-Jaipur, NFR-Maligaon/Guwahati, NR-New Delhi, SCR-Secunderabad, SECR-Bilaspur, SER-Kolkata, SWR-Hubli, SR-Chennai, WCR-Jabalpur, WR-Mumbai.

Passengers must board and alight from trains only when the train is at a complete standstill and at the platform side. Attempting to board or alight from a moving train is dangerous and a punishable offense under the Railways Act.

Railway emergency helpline: 139 (for security, medical emergency, cleanliness, or any other complaints). Rail Madad portal and app for registering complaints. Women helpline on trains: 182. RPF helpline: 1800-111-322 (toll-free).

Children below 5 years of age travel free on Indian Railways without a separate berth or seat. Children aged 5-12 years are charged approximately half the adult fare and are allotted a seat/berth. Children above 12 years are charged full fare.

Pets (dogs/cats) can be carried in First AC (1A) or First Class (FC) by booking the entire cabin/coupe. Small pets may be carried in baskets in some coaches with prior permission. Booking is facilitated through the Indian Railways parcel website. Guide dogs for visually impaired passengers are allowed in all classes.

rule_title: Confirmed Ticket Clerkage Charges by Class
rule_description: Clerkage (flat cancellation) charges per passenger for confirmed tickets: 1A/EC: Rs.240, 2A: Rs.200, 3A: Rs.180, CC: Rs.180, FC: Rs.200, SL: Rs.120, 2S: Rs.60. GST is additionally applicable for AC classes.

rule_title: RAC and WL Ticket Clerkage Charges
rule_description: Clerkage charges per passenger for RAC and Waitlisted tickets are a flat Rs.60 for all classes (1A/EC, 2A, 3A, CC, FC, SL, 2S).
If a confirmed ticket is cancelled more than 48 hours before the scheduled departure of the train, the passenger receives a full refund minus the flat clerkage charges applicable for the class.
If a confirmed ticket is cancelled between 12 hours and 48 hours before departure, 25% of the base fare or the clerkage charges (whichever is higher) is deducted from the refund.
If a confirmed ticket is cancelled between 4 hours and 12 hours before departure, 50% of the base fare or the clerkage charges (whichever is higher) is deducted from the refund.
If a confirmed ticket is not cancelled before chart preparation, no refund is admissible. For trains with early morning departure, charting is done 6 to 8 hours before departure.
If Indian Railways cancels a train, all passengers automatically receive a full refund without any deduction. No TDR or manual action required.
If a train is running late by more than 3 hours, passengers can file a TDR (Ticket Deposit Receipt) before the departure of the train from charting station for a full refund.
If the AC system in a coach is not functioning, passengers are entitled to a refund of the difference in fare between the AC class and the corresponding non-AC class.
If a train is diverted from its original route and the passenger's destination is not covered, the passenger can file a TDR within 72 hours for a full refund.

rule_title: Seat Given in Lower Class Refund
rule_description: If a passenger holding a higher class ticket is accommodated in a lower class, they are entitled to a refund of the difference in fare between the two classes.
If a seat or berth is not provided to a passenger despite having a confirmed ticket, the passenger is entitled to a full refund.

TDR (Ticket Deposit Receipt) is filed online through the IRCTC website or app when the normal cancellation window has passed. The e-ticket TDR refund process takes at least 90 days. For PRS counter tickets, TDR can be filed at the station and refund is processed to the counter of origin.
If a ticket is not cancelled but the passenger does not travel, and cancellation is done up to 30 minutes before the train's departure (before charting), a full refund less clerkage charges is given.

GNWL (General Waiting List) is issued when you book from the train's starting station or a major station near the origin. This is the most common type and has the highest priority for confirmation among all waiting list types.

RLWL (Remote Location Waiting List) is issued for intermediate stations that are important stops along the route. These stations have a smaller fixed quota of seats, so confirmation chances are lower than GNWL.

PQWL (Pooled Quota Waiting List) is issued for passengers traveling between intermediate stations (often shorter distances). It has a very limited pool of seats and lower confirmation chances compared to GNWL.

TQWL (Tatkal Waiting List) is issued when booking under the Tatkal quota. It has the lowest priority for confirmation. If not confirmed by chart preparation, the ticket is automatically cancelled and refunded.

RSWL (Roadside Station Waiting List) is issued for journeys from the originating station to a roadside station or between small intermediate stations. It has very low confirmation chances as it relies on small specific quotas.
The order of priority for waitlist confirmation is: GNWL (highest) > RLWL (moderate) > PQWL (low) > TQWL (very low) > RSWL (lowest). RAC has higher priority than any waiting list type.

After the final reservation chart is prepared, any remaining waitlisted e-tickets are automatically cancelled and refunded. Passengers cannot board the train with a fully waitlisted e-ticket. However, if at least one passenger on the same PNR has confirmed or RAC status, all passengers on that PNR can board.

If you miss your train with a confirmed ticket, there is no automatic refund. You must file a TDR (Ticket Deposit Receipt) online through the IRCTC website or app. Filing a TDR does not guarantee a refund — Indian Railways reviews each request. You cannot use the existing ticket to board another train; a new ticket must be purchased.
If you miss your train with an RAC ticket, similar rules apply as confirmed tickets. You must file a TDR. The chances of refund approval may depend on the specific circumstances of the case.
If your ticket is still waitlisted and you miss the train, the e-ticket would have been auto-cancelled after charting anyway. The refund is processed automatically minus clerkage charges.

rule_title: Missed Connecting Train Due to Delay
rule_description: If you have a single PNR for a connecting journey and you miss the second connecting train because the first train was late, you may be eligible for a refund for the untraveled portion. Surrender your ticket at the station or file a TDR.

Standard fixed fare applies to most Mail/Express trains. The fare is calculated based on distance, class of travel, and train type. Fare includes base fare plus reservation charge, superfast charge (if applicable), and GST for AC classes.

Tatkal tickets carry additional premium charges over the base fare. The tatkal charges are a fixed percentage of the base fare: AC classes: 10% to 30% of base fare (minimum Rs.90, maximum Rs.500). Sleeper: 10% to 30% (minimum Rs.90, maximum Rs.200).

Premium Tatkal uses dynamic pricing where fares increase based on demand. Only available through IRCTC website/app (not agents or counters). Only confirmed tickets are issued — no RAC or waitlist. Generally non-refundable.

Dynamic/Flexi fare applies to premium trains like Rajdhani, Shatabdi, Duronto, Vande Bharat, and Tejas. The base fare increases by 10% incrementally as seats are sold, up to a predetermined ceiling (typically 1.5x the base fare). Sleeper class and lower classes are exempted from dynamic pricing.

Suvidha trains are special trains with market-linked dynamic pricing. Fares start at a higher base and increase with demand. Available only during festival/peak seasons. No concessions or passes are accepted.

Suburban trains (local trains) operate on a separate fare structure with nominal pricing. Season tickets (monthly/quarterly passes) are available at heavily discounted rates. No fare increase was applied to suburban services in the 2025 fare rationalization.

General reservation tickets can be booked up to 60 days in advance online via IRCTC or at PRS counters. Online booking opens at 08:00 AM IST. Counter booking hours vary by station. Multiple passengers can be booked on a single PNR (up to 6 passengers per PNR for online booking).

Ladies Quota (LD) berths are earmarked in Sleeper Class (SL) and Second Sitting (2S) coaches on many long-distance Mail/Express trains. Available for women of all ages traveling alone or with children. Male family members can travel in the same coach if booked together on the same PNR.

Senior citizens (men 60+ and women 58+) can book under the Senior Citizen/Lower Berth quota (SS). This provides automatic allotment of lower berths subject to availability. The former fare concession (40% for men, 50% for women) was suspended in March 2020 and has not been reinstated as of 2026.

Students can avail fare concessions for travel between hometown and place of education (during vacations), attending government-organized camps/seminars, and appearing in competitive exams (UPSC, SSC). Requires bonafide certificate from recognized institution. Applicable for students up to age 25. Must be processed at PRS counters.

Foreign tourists and NRIs with valid passports can book under Foreign Tourist Quota (FTQ). Booking available up to 365 days in advance. Passport number and nationality required at booking. Original passport and valid visa must be carried during journey. Failure to produce documents attracts penalty equivalent to 3x the fare. Payment accepted via international debit/credit cards.

Passengers with confirmed or RAC tickets can change their boarding station up to 30 minutes before the scheduled departure. Can be done online (IRCTC) or at PRS counters. Change allowed only once. Not available for VIKALP, current booking, or I-Tickets. Once changed, original boarding point right is forfeited.

Circular journey tickets allow a passenger to travel on a round trip visiting multiple stations. The ticket is issued at PRS counters for a planned itinerary. Valid for the specified route only. Break of journey is allowed at intermediate stations as per the itinerary.

UTS mobile app allows booking of unreserved (general class) tickets. Tickets can also be purchased at station counters or via ATVM machines. Important: Booking via UTS app inside railway station premises is not permitted — tickets must be purchased before entering the station area.

rule_title: Payment Deducted But Ticket Not Booked
rule_description: If money is deducted from your account but the ticket is not booked, the amount is automatically refunded to the source account within 5-7 working days. If not refunded, file a complaint through the IRCTC customer care or RailMadad portal. Do not attempt to rebook immediately as this may result in double deduction.

Passengers can check live train running status through: NTES (National Train Enquiry System) at enquiry.indianrail.gov.in, erail.in, the RailOne app, or by calling the 139 helpline. SMS-based enquiry is also available by sending SPOT <train_number> to 139.

Passengers can request coach cleaning by sending SMS to 58888 with format: CLEAN <10-digit PNR>. Can also be raised through the RailMadad app or portal. Request is forwarded to On Board Housekeeping Service (OBHS) staff for immediate action.

If facing issues during the journey (unauthorized occupants, berth disputes, cleanliness, missing amenities), approach the TTE (Travelling Ticket Examiner) of your coach. If the TTE is unresponsive, note their name/badge number and file a complaint through RailMadad app or call 139.

Passengers can tweet complaints to @RailMinIndia or @RailwaySeva on Twitter/X for quick response. Include PNR number, coach number, and train details. For food-related complaints, tag @IRCATERING. Response time is typically within 30-60 minutes during working hours.

For medical emergencies on train, dial 138 (medical helpline) or 139 (general helpline). Inform the TTE or any onboard staff immediately. Staff can access the Doctor Onboard list, provide first aid from the medical box, or coordinate with the next station to arrange a medical team or ambulance.

Do not travel on footboard or roof of trains. Do not lean out of windows or doors. Keep luggage under your seat or on designated racks. Lock your bags securely, especially at night. Do not accept food or drinks from strangers. Do not pull the alarm chain without valid reason.

Food can be ordered on train through: IRCTC e-catering portal (ecatering.irctc.co.in), the RailOne app, or by calling 1323. Delivery is available at select stations on the route. Menu and pricing are displayed on the e-catering portal. Payment can be made online or cash on delivery.

RailMadad (railmadad.indianrailways.gov.in) is the primary centralized grievance redressal portal for all train and station-related issues. Available as website and mobile app. Requires PNR or UTS number to register. Complaints are tracked with a unique ID and escalated automatically if unresolved.

139 is a 24x7 centralized helpline for: security concerns, medical emergencies, cleanliness issues, general complaints, and train enquiries. Available in multiple languages. Integrated with RailMadad for complaint tracking.
Complaints can be sent via SMS to 9717630982. Include PNR/UTS number and brief description. Also used for CLEAN requests (SMS CLEAN <PNR> to 58888) and train enquiry.

For security issues: RPF handles railway property protection and passenger safety. GRP (Government Railway Police) handles criminal matters (theft, robbery, assault). RPF helpline: 182. For theft on train, contact TTE/Guard/RPF on board. FIR forms are available with onboard staff — fill and hand back, no need to break journey.

If theft occurs on train: immediately inform the TTE, Guard, or RPF personnel on board. On-board staff carry FIR forms — fill one out and hand it back. No need to break your journey to file an FIR. Call RPF helpline 182 or general helpline 139. File formal complaint through RailMadad portal.

If paper ticket is lost: Before charting — get duplicate ticket from PRS counter by paying small clerkage fee. After charting — pay 50% of fare for duplicate ticket. E-tickets can be shown from the IRCTC app. No refund is provided for lost tickets but duplicate allows journey completion. Report loss immediately to prevent fraudulent use.

CPGRAMS (Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System) at pgportal.gov.in handles broader public grievances. For specific train/station issues, RailMadad is recommended for faster resolution. CPGRAMS is suitable for policy-level or systemic complaints.

Coach Attendant must report 30-60 minutes before departure. Ensures all internal fittings (lights, fans, reading lamps, charging sockets, water supply) are operational. Pre-cools AC coaches and ensures bedrolls availability. Carries passenger list and assists with berth queries. Coordinates cleaning with safaiwalas. Monitors AC plant parameters every 2 hours. Attends to minor electrical/mechanical issues.

The Train Superintendent (Train Captain) is the overall in-charge of onboard services on premium and long-distance trains. All personnel (TTEs, coach attendants, catering staff, OBHS housekeeping) report to the Train Captain. Responsible for service quality, addressing passenger complaints, ensuring staff are in proper uniform with name badges. Prepares trip report at end of journey. Primary point of contact during emergencies.

Catering services managed by IRCTC through pantry cars and static units. Staff must display printed menu cards with prices. IRCTC supervisors monitor food safety and hygiene. Must follow FSSAI standards. Passengers can report catering issues via 138 helpline or @IRCATERING on Twitter.

OBHS (Onboard Housekeeping Services) staff handle continuous cleaning of cabins, toilets, and washbasins during the journey. Coordinated with CTS (Clean Train Station) scheme. Real-time monitoring is implemented for service quality. Passengers can request cleaning via SMS 58888 or RailMadad app.

General Quota (GN) is the largest allocation in any train. Booking opens 60 days in advance at 08:00 AM for online and at counter opening hours for PRS. No additional charges over base fare. Available for all classes. Can result in GNWL if seats are full.

Lower berth quota is for: Senior citizens (men 60+ and women 58+), persons with disabilities, and pregnant women. Automatic allotment of lower berths subject to availability. Can be requested at time of booking. If lower berth not available, regular berth is allotted.

Persons with Disabilities (PwD) can book under the Handicapped Quota with valid disability certificate from government hospital. Fare concession of 50-75% depending on disability type. Escort allowed to travel at same concession rate. Both lower berth preference and wheelchair assistance available.

eRail.in is a popular third-party Indian Railways enquiry platform providing: PNR status check, live train running status, train schedule and route, seat availability, fare enquiry, train statistics, and coach composition. Free to use. Available in English and Hindi.

Railway information available via SMS: Send SPOT <train_no> to 139 for running status. PNR status: Send PNR <10-digit PNR> to 139. Seat availability: Call 139 and follow voice prompts. Available 24x7.

Coach layout and composition details available on erail.in and the NTES app. Shows berth numbers, door positions, and class arrangement. Useful for planning boarding position on platform. Layout varies by train type (Rajdhani, Shatabdi, Mail/Express, Vande Bharat).

Rajdhani Express trains connect major cities to New Delhi. Fully air-conditioned. Dynamic (flexi) fare applies. Meals are included in the ticket price. Premium service with priority for punctuality. Available classes: 1A, 2A, 3A. All stops are limited for faster journey.

Shatabdi Express trains are day-travel premium trains connecting cities within 500-700 km distance. Only chair car classes (CC and EC). Return journey the same day. Dynamic fare applies. Meals included. No overnight travel.

Vande Bharat Express is India's premium semi-high-speed train. Maximum speed of 160 kmph. Available classes: CC (Chair Car) and EC (Executive Chair Car). Dynamic fare applies. Complimentary meals included. If delayed by 2+ hours, additional complimentary meals provided. Automatic doors, bio-vacuum toilets, and WiFi onboard.

Duronto Express trains are non-stop (zero halt) trains between major cities. Available in AC classes (1A, 2A, 3A) and Sleeper. Dynamic fare applies for AC classes. Meals included for AC passengers. One of the fastest train categories on the network.

Garib Rath Express offers AC 3-Tier travel at significantly reduced fares compared to Rajdhani/Shatabdi. Designed for budget-conscious AC travelers. No bedding provided. Limited stops for faster journey. Available classes: 3A only.

Jan Shatabdi Express is an economical version of Shatabdi Express. Day travel inter-city service. Available in both AC (CC) and non-AC (2S) classes. Meals not included in ticket price but available for purchase. Fixed fare (no dynamic pricing).

Passenger trains stop at all stations on the route. Slowest category. General unreserved tickets. No reservation required. Monthly/quarterly season tickets available. Fare is the cheapest among all train types.

Foreign tourists should: carry original passport and visa at all times during train travel. Use the Foreign Tourist Quota for easier booking. Keep valuables secure and use chain-locks for bags. Avoid traveling in general/unreserved class on long routes. Pre-book meals through e-catering. Download IRCTC and RailOne apps for live updates.

Women travelers can book under Ladies Quota for Sleeper/2S classes. Ladies-only coaches available on suburban trains. RPF women helpline: 182. Avoid general compartments on long-distance trains at night. Emergency alarm chain available in all coaches. Many stations have women-only waiting rooms.

Tourist information centers are available at major railway stations. Provide information about train services, local tourism, hotel booking, and guided tours. Many stations have special tourist assistance counters. Pre-paid taxi services available at most major stations.

Travel agents must register through IRCTC's authorized agent portal. Agents are barred from booking Tatkal tickets during the first 30 minutes of the booking window. Agents must display their valid license at their booking counter. Commission structure varies by class and ticket type.

IRCTC agents have specific booking limits per day. Only one Tatkal ticket per IRCTC user ID in first 30 minutes of booking window. Agents must verify passenger identity for certain quota bookings. Unauthorized bulk booking or use of scripts/bots results in permanent ban.

Under the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act 2026 effective from June 20 2026, the minimum penalty for ticketless travel is Rs.500 plus the applicable fare and excess charges. Repeat offenders face higher fines. Fines are subject to a 10% increase every three years.

rule_title: Penalty for Using Another Person's Ticket
rule_description: Travelling on another person's ticket attracts a penalty of Rs.500 plus forfeiture of the ticket. Both the original ticket holder and the person using the ticket may face action.

Smoking in trains or railway premises now attracts a penalty of Rs.2000 (increased from Rs.200) under the revised 2026 penalty framework. Applies to all enclosed areas including AC coaches, waiting rooms, platforms, and station buildings.
Unauthorized hawking or begging on trains and railway premises attracts a fine of Rs.2000. Persistent offenders may face imprisonment of up to one year.
Creating nuisance, abusive language, or drunkenness on trains or railway premises: Rs.1000 for first offence and up to Rs.5000 for repeat offenders. May also result in imprisonment.

Unauthorized entry or trespassing on railway tracks, bridges, or restricted railway premises attracts a penalty of Rs.500. Railway property damage leads to additional charges under the Railway Property (Unlawful Possession) Act.

rule_title: Penalty for Men in Women's Reserved Coach
rule_description: Male passengers found travelling in coaches or compartments reserved for women face a penalty of Rs.2500 under the 2026 revised penalty framework. This includes ladies-only coaches in suburban/local trains and ladies quota berths in long-distance trains.

Carrying dangerous or prohibited goods (explosives, inflammable materials, corrosive substances, gas cylinders, acids) on passenger trains attracts a minimum penalty of Rs.10000. The goods are confiscated and the passenger may face criminal prosecution.
Obstructing or assaulting railway staff on duty attracts a penalty of Rs.2500. This includes TTE, RPF, Station Master, Guard, and any other railway employee performing their duties.
General disobedience of railway rules (Section 166 of Railways Act) attracts Rs.2000 for first offence and Rs.5000 for repeat violations under the 2026 amendment.

Railway penalties are generally collected on the spot by authorized railway staff (TTE, RPF). A receipt must be provided to the passenger. Failure to pay the penalty can result in the matter being referred to a competent court for further legal action.

Many local, suburban, and passenger trains feature dedicated coaches exclusively for women. Male passengers travelling in these coaches face a penalty of Rs.2500. In long-distance trains, specific berths are earmarked under the Ladies Quota in Sleeper and Second Sitting classes.

In Sleeper Class, typically 6 berths per train are earmarked for women under Ladies Quota. In AC coaches (2A/3A), a combined quota with senior citizens provides 2-4 lower berths per coach. Women travelling with male children under 12 can also be accommodated under this quota.

Meri Saheli is a pan-India initiative by the Railway Protection Force (RPF) focused on safety and security of female passengers travelling alone. RPF personnel escort solo women travellers covering their journey from originating station to destination. Women can request Meri Saheli assistance through the 182 helpline or RPF personnel at the station.

Women's safety helplines: 182 (dedicated RPF women's helpline, toll-free, 24x7), 139 (general railway helpline for all security assistance), 138 (non-emergency issues). Women can also tweet @RPaborad or @RailMinIndia for help.

rule_title: Safety Tips for Solo Women Travellers
rule_description: Book tickets in advance via IRCTC. Opt for upper berths for privacy and security. Plan journeys to arrive during daylight hours. Share travel details and live location with family or friends. Be cautious about sharing personal information with strangers. Carry safety tools like pepper spray for emergencies. Seek help from TTE or RPF if uncomfortable.

During night travel: ensure vestibule doors are locked (TTE responsibility). Use the curtains provided on berths. Keep phone charged and within reach. Emergency alarm chain is available in all coaches. Do not accept food or drinks from strangers. Report any suspicious activity to TTE or RPF immediately.

UTS (Unreserved Ticketing System) on Mobile app is the official platform for booking unreserved, platform, and season tickets. Registration requires mobile number and personal details. Two booking modes: Paperless (GPS-enabled, ticket on phone) and Paper (receive Booking ID, print at ATVM/counter). Booking via UTS inside station premises is NOT permitted.

Paperless tickets booked via UTS app: GPS must be enabled and you must be outside station premises/tracks to book. Journey must commence within 1 hour of booking. No printout needed — show ticket on phone to checking staff. If phone battery dies and ticket cannot be shown, treated as travelling without ticket.

Paper tickets booked via UTS app: receive a Booking ID in the app. Must visit source station and print ticket from ATVM (Automatic Ticket Vending Machine) or counter before boarding. Cannot board train without printed ticket.

SMS services are for ENQUIRY only — cannot book tickets via SMS. Available services: Send SPOT <train_no> to 139 for running status. Send PNR <10-digit number> to 139 for PNR status. Call 139 for seat availability and general enquiry. erail.in is for information only, not for booking.

eRail.in is a third-party information portal and does not handle any ticket booking or payment. It collects minimal user data for analytics. No personal railway booking information is stored. The platform provides train enquiry, PNR status check, and schedule information sourced from Indian Railways public data. Not affiliated with Indian Railways or IRCTC officially.

rule_title: Airport Transfer Services at Railway Stations
rule_description: Many major railway stations offer pre-paid taxi and cab services for airport transfers. Services include Ola, Uber, and state transport bus connections. Some stations like New Delhi, Mumbai CST, and Chennai Central have dedicated airport shuttle counters. Pre-booking through apps is recommended. Auto-rickshaw and metro connectivity is also available at major stations for airport access.

Indian Railways runs special festival trains during peak seasons including Diwali, Holi, Chhath Puja, Christmas, and summer holidays. These are temporary additional services to handle extra passenger load. Booking opens closer to the festival dates. Suvidha trains with dynamic pricing are commonly deployed during festivals. Foreign tourists can plan visits during major Indian festivals like Republic Day (Jan 26), Independence Day (Aug 15), Diwali, Holi, and Durga Puja for unique cultural experiences.

rule_title: Airport to Railway Station Transfer Guide for Tourists
rule_description: Foreign tourists arriving at Indian airports can reach railway stations via: Pre-paid taxi counters inside airport terminals, metro rail (available in Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai, Kolkata, Hyderabad), app-based cabs (Ola/Uber), and airport shuttle buses. New Delhi Railway Station is connected to the Airport Express Metro Line. Mumbai CST is accessible via local trains from the airport area. Pre-booking transport is recommended for first-time visitors.
